stemHtml: What would be the safest course of action when approaching a mountain range at a right angle, with the mountain tops reaching 10 000 ft and the planned flight altitude set at 12 000 ft, while observing lenticular clouds above the mountain range?

options.a: Lense shaped clouds (Ac Lenticularis) indicate strong vertical currents with tendency to development of CB clouds; the safest procedure is to increase obstacle clearance before proceeding as planned.
options.b: Lense shaped clouds (Ac Lenticularis) indicate smooth air overhead and downwind from the mountain range; flight should be proceeded as planned while maintaining obstacle clearance.
options.c: Lense shaped clouds (Ac Lenticularis) indicate mountain waves at high levels, and rotor zones at lower levels, so safest procedure would be to avoid the area and re-route around it.
options.d: Lense shaped clouds (Ac Lenticularis) indicate turbulent air overhead and downwind from the mountain range; the flight should be proceeded at minimum safe altitude.
